purpose: Records the maintained regression coverage that asserts non-empty accessibility labels on the 5 root tab buttons under each of en/de/ar/ja/ru. XCUITest reads XCUIElement.label which is the value VoiceOver announces, so a non-empty assertion guards the announcement contract without requiring a real VoiceOver-listening human pass.

test_run.build_commit: d8fbec4 (pre-this-slice baseline; this slice's regression methods land in the next commit)

claim: Each of en/de/ar/ja/ru launches Owlory in the target locale, the root tab bar exposes exactly 5 buttons, and every one of those buttons reports a non-empty XCUIElement.label value at the time of launch.
not_claimed: a real VoiceOver-listening human pass occurred (this is XCUITest-only)
non-tab-bar accessibility labels are covered (Today rows, Continue items, train sessions, write notes, etc.)
the labels are idiomatic VoiceOver phrasing (only that they are non-empty)
Dynamic Type at AccessibilityXL is verified for VoiceOver users specifically (the AccessibilityXL shell-settle tests are separate)
iPadOS or macOS VoiceOver behavior is covered (iPhone 17 simulator only)
rationale_for_chosen_locales: Selected en as source, de as native-reviewed, ar as RTL, ja as CJK katakana-heavy, ru as long Cyrillic. Together they cover the four bucket-gate flavours plus the source baseline. Adding more locales would multiply test runtime; the assertion shape is locale-agnostic so the additional coverage value tapers off after one representative per bucket.
follow_up_recommendations: If a future regression adds non-empty AX-label assertions for non-tab-bar surfaces (Today rows, Continue items), update this manifest with the new method names.

A real VoiceOver-listening human pass is a separate proof track and should land under a `voiceover-human-listening-pass` slice if and when one is queued; that's NOT a precondition for the hig-ui-reviewed claim per the 2026-05-18 policy.
